2026-04-21T09:00:54.5508145Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-21T09:00:54.5508705Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-21T09:00:54.5509123Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-21T09:00:54.5509482Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-21T09:00:54.5509787Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-21T09:00:54.5510018Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-21T09:00:54.5510173Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-21T09:00:54.5510350Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-21T09:00:54.5510513Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-21T09:00:54.5510734Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-21T09:00:54.5511215Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-21T09:00:54.5511466Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"state_reason":{"description":"The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.","enum":["COMPLETED","NOT_PLANNED","DUPLICATE"],"type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"close_issue"},{"description":"Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_labels"},{"description":"Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"assignee":{"description":"Single GitHub username to assign. This package reached its first stable GA (`1.0.0`) in July 2025 and has since been shipping without meaningful documentation improvements to match the stable API surface.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The **\\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34;** section contains only a generic link to Azure SDK .NET guidelines — it has no explanation of CarbonOptimization-specific concepts (e.g., what carbon emissions data is, how subscriptions/tenants are scoped, what query types exist)\\n- The **\\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34;** section contains only a link to generic Azure SDK samples — there are **no inline code snippets** demonstrating how to query carbon emissions data\\n- No `tests/Samples/` directory exists; therefore there are **no test-backed `#region Snippet:` snippets** that the `Update-Snippets.ps1` tool could inject into the README\\n- The auto-generated samples in `samples/Generated/Samples/Sample_TenantResourceExtensions.cs` cover the full API surface but are not referenced from the README\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/carbon/Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization/README.md` — **edit**\\n\\nReplace the stub \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThe Carbon Optimization library lets you query carbon emissions data for Azure resources scoped to a tenant or subscription. Key concepts include:\\n\\n- **Carbon emissions query:** Send a `CarbonEmissionDataAvailableDateRange` request to discover the date range for which data is available, then submit a `QueryCarbonEmissionReport` request to retrieve aggregated emissions data.\\n- **Query scope:** Queries operate at the tenant level via `TenantResource.GetCarbonService()` — no per-subscription resource creation is required.\\n- **Report granularity:** Results can be broken down by subscription, resource group, resource type, location, or resource, and aggregated over a date range.\\n- **Data models:** Responses use strongly-typed model classes such as `CarbonEmissionDataAvailableDateRangeResponse`, `CarbonEmissionListResult`, and `CarbonEmissionItem`.\\n```\\n\\nReplace the stub \\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; section with:\\n\\n````markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\nThe following sections show several code snippets demonstrating common patterns used in the CarbonOptimization client library.\\n\\n### Get available date range\\n\\n```C# Snippet:CarbonOptimization_GetAvailableDateRange\\n```\\n\\n### Query carbon emission report\\n\\n```C# Snippet:CarbonOptimization_QueryReport\\n```\\n````\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nCreate a new test sample file at `sdk/carbon/Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization/tests/Samples/Sample1_CarbonOptimization.cs` with the following content:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization.Models;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample1_CarbonOptimization : SamplesBase\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [AsyncOnly]\\n        public async Task GetAvailableDateRange()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CarbonOptimization_GetAvailableDateRange\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            var tenantResource = client.GetTenants().GetAllAsync().FirstAsync().Result;\\n            var carbonService = tenantResource.GetCarbonService();\\n\\n            var response = await carbonService.GetCarbonEmissionDataAvailableDateRangeAsync();\\n            CarbonEmissionDataAvailableDateRangeResponse dateRange = response.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Data available from {dateRange.StartDate} to {dateRange.EndDate}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [AsyncOnly]\\n        public async Task QueryCarbonEmissionReport()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CarbonOptimization_QueryReport\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            var tenantResource = client.GetTenants().GetAllAsync().FirstAsync().Result;\\n            var carbonService = tenantResource.GetCarbonService();\\n\\n            var queryParameters = new OverallSummaryReportQueryParameters(\\n                reportType: CarbonEmissionQueryReportType.OverallSummaryReport,\\n                dateRange: new CarbonEmissionQueryDateRange(\\n                    start: new DateTimeOffset(2024, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0, TimeSpan.Zero),\\n                    end: new DateTimeOffset(2024, 12, 31, 0, 0, 0, TimeSpan.Zero)));\\n\\n            await foreach (CarbonEmissionItem item in carbonService.QueryCarbonEmissionReportsAsync(queryParameters))\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Subscription: {item.SubscriptionId}, Emissions: {item.TotalCarbonEmission} kgCO2e\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**Snippet names:**\\n- `CarbonOptimization_GetAvailableDateRange` — maps to the \\u0026#34;Get available date range\\u0026#34; example block in README\\n- `CarbonOptimization_QueryReport` — maps to the \\u0026#34;Query carbon emission report\\u0026#34; example block in README\\n\\nThe `Update-Snippets.ps1` script will read `#region Snippet:` tags and inject the code into the README placeholder fences automatically.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/carbon/Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization/README.md` currently has all required sections, but the following need content changes:\\n\\n1. **Key concepts** — This package reached its first stable GA (`1.0.0`) in July 2025 and has since been shipping without meaningful documentation improvements to match the stable API surface.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The **\\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34;** section contains only a generic link to Azure SDK .NET guidelines — it has no explanation of CarbonOptimization-specific concepts (e.g., what carbon emissions data is, how subscriptions/tenants are scoped, what query types exist)\\n- The **\\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34;** section contains only a link to generic Azure SDK samples — there are **no inline code snippets** demonstrating how to query carbon emissions data\\n- No `tests/Samples/` directory exists; therefore there are **no test-backed `#region Snippet:` snippets** that the `Update-Snippets.ps1` tool could inject into the README\\n- The auto-generated samples in `samples/Generated/Samples/Sample_TenantResourceExtensions.cs` cover the full API surface but are not referenced from the README\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/carbon/Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization/README.md` — **edit**\\n\\nReplace the stub \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThe Carbon Optimization library lets you query carbon emissions data for Azure resources scoped to a tenant or subscription. Key concepts include:\\n\\n- **Carbon emissions query:** Send a `CarbonEmissionDataAvailableDateRange` request to discover the date range for which data is available, then submit a `QueryCarbonEmissionReport` request to retrieve aggregated emissions data.\\n- **Query scope:** Queries operate at the tenant level via `TenantResource.GetCarbonService()` — no per-subscription resource creation is required.\\n- **Report granularity:** Results can be broken down by subscription, resource group, resource type, location, or resource, and aggregated over a date range.\\n- **Data models:** Responses use strongly-typed model classes such as `CarbonEmissionDataAvailableDateRangeResponse`, `CarbonEmissionListResult`, and `CarbonEmissionItem`.\\n```\\n\\nReplace the stub \\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; section with:\\n\\n````markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\nThe following sections show several code snippets demonstrating common patterns used in the CarbonOptimization client library.\\n\\n### Get available date range\\n\\n```C# Snippet:CarbonOptimization_GetAvailableDateRange\\n```\\n\\n### Query carbon emission report\\n\\n```C# Snippet:CarbonOptimization_QueryReport\\n```\\n````\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nCreate a new test sample file at `sdk/carbon/Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization/tests/Samples/Sample1_CarbonOptimization.cs` with the following content:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization.Models;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample1_CarbonOptimization : SamplesBase\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [AsyncOnly]\\n        public async Task GetAvailableDateRange()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CarbonOptimization_GetAvailableDateRange\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            var tenantResource = client.GetTenants().GetAllAsync().FirstAsync().Result;\\n            var carbonService = tenantResource.GetCarbonService();\\n\\n            var response = await carbonService.GetCarbonEmissionDataAvailableDateRangeAsync();\\n            CarbonEmissionDataAvailableDateRangeResponse dateRange = response.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Data available from {dateRange.StartDate} to {dateRange.EndDate}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [AsyncOnly]\\n        public async Task QueryCarbonEmissionReport()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CarbonOptimization_QueryReport\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            var tenantResource = client.GetTenants().GetAllAsync().FirstAsync().Result;\\n            var carbonService = tenantResource.GetCarbonService();\\n\\n            var queryParameters = new OverallSummaryReportQueryParameters(\\n                reportType: CarbonEmissionQueryReportType.OverallSummaryReport,\\n                dateRange: new CarbonEmissionQueryDateRange(\\n                    start: new DateTimeOffset(2024, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0, TimeSpan.Zero),\\n                    end: new DateTimeOffset(2024, 12, 31, 0, 0, 0, TimeSpan.Zero)));\\n\\n            await foreach (CarbonEmissionItem item in carbonService.QueryCarbonEmissionReportsAsync(queryParameters))\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Subscription: {item.SubscriptionId}, Emissions: {item.TotalCarbonEmission} kgCO2e\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**Snippet names:**\\n- `CarbonOptimization_GetAvailableDateRange` — maps to the \\u0026#34;Get available date range\\u0026#34; example block in README\\n- `CarbonOptimization_QueryReport` — maps to the \\u0026#34;Query carbon emission report\\u0026#34; example block in README\\n\\nThe `Update-Snippets.ps1` script will read `#region Snippet:` tags and inject the code into the README placeholder fences automatically.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/carbon/Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization/README.md` currently has all required sections, but the following need content changes:\\n\\n1. **Key concepts** — 1.13.0\\n\\n### Describe the bug\\n\\nWe are using the SDK to delete different kinds of resources (VMs, KVs, disks, etc.):\\n```\\nawait resource.DeleteAsync(WaitUntil.Started);\\n```\\nToday this call started to fail consistently with 400 Bad Request when deleting public IPs, network security groups and network interface cards. The error mentions a missing resource provider for a recent API version, but the resources were created via an ARM template using `2020-07-01`, while the SDK doesn't expose which version it uses.\\n\\n### Expected behavior\\n\\nThe method call to succeed.\\n\\n### Actual behavior\\n\\nWhen deleting PIPs (same for NSGs and NICs) we get the following error message:\\n```\\nNo registered resource provider found for location 'westeurope' and API version '2025-03-01' for type 'publicIPAddresses'.
